name: GT-bh-cstar
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:458-466 (1.3.2), VERBATIM: "$\mathscr{A}$ is called a Banach algebra if $\|xy\| \le \|x\| \|y\|$ for all $x, y \in \mathscr{A}$." and "If H is a complex Hilbert space then B(H), the bounded linear operators on H, is a $C^*$ algebra in the usual operator norm". So B(H) is a C*-algebra and V=B(H)_sa its self-adjoint part.
